data.author: Tiryoh
data.message:
Update Rosbag2 filename format to index+name+timestamp (#2265)

* Update rosbag2 filename format to index+name+timestamp

Change rosbag2 bag filename format to {counter}_{prefix}_{timestamp}.
Extract prefix from directory name by removing timestamp pattern,
and use file creation timestamp instead of directory timestamp
to avoid duplicate timestamps in filenames.

- Update sequential writer and reindexer to generate filenames with index+name+timestamp format
- Update and refactor filename testing

Signed-off-by: Daisuke Sato <tiryoh@gmail.com>

* Address review comments

- Consolidate timestamp generation in sequential_writer.cpp
- Rename reindexer regex members to descriptive names with const/inline init
- Create shared bag_files_helpers.hpp in rosbag2_test_common
- Move test helpers into fixture classes (sequential_writer, compression_writer)
- Remove duplicate/unused helper functions across test files
- Simplify filename pattern checks by letting helpers handle path stripping
- Revert test_rosbag2_info_end_to_end.cpp (fixed test resources, no changes needed)
- Simplify bag file counting and wait_for_storage_file in E2E tests

Signed-off-by: Daisuke Sato <tiryoh@gmail.com>

* Delegate record_fixture wait helpers to shared bag_files_helpers

Signed-off-by: Daisuke Sato <tiryoh@gmail.com>

* Fix flaky split test by waiting for expected number of storage files

The splitting_bagsize test was stopping the recorder after only 1
storage file appeared, before splits could occur. Extract
count_storage_files() and generalize wait_for_storage_files(count)
so the test waits for all expected splits before stopping.

Signed-off-by: Daisuke Sato <tiryoh@gmail.com>

* Fixes after the second round of review from MichaelOrlov

- Mostly minor changes in tests and helper functions to improve code
 readability and maintainability.

Signed-off-by: Michael Orlov <morlovmr@gmail.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Daisuke Sato <tiryoh@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Michael Orlov <morlovmr@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Michael Orlov <morlovmr@gmail.com>

data.author: luke-alloy
data.message:
Feature: Circular logging by split count (--max-bag-files) (#2218)

* WIP max-record-size and max-record-duration parameters

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Add circular logging by split count

- Add StorageOptions.max_splits (+ YAML/params, Python bindings)
- CLI: --max-splits (before size/duration)
- Writer: delete oldest files when exceeding size, duration, or split count

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Code clean up

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Added unit test for sequential_writer, lint fix

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Cleaned test_sequential_writter to fix cpplint test failures

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Fixed _storage.pyi unit test

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* removed unnecessary files

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Remove max_record_size and max_record_duration options

Simplify storage options by removing max_record_size and
max_record_duration fields and CLI arguments. Circular logging now
only supports limiting by split count (max_splits).

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Clean up unused code in sequential_writer

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* refactor: rename max_splits to max_bag_files

    Replace max_splits with max_bag_files across all interfaces (CLI, C++,
    Python, YAML, ROS params) for improved clarity. Addressed suggested code changes

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Simplify delete_oldest_files_if_needed loop logic

Remove redundant size check by directly comparing against max_bag_files
in the while loop condition.

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Add integration test for max_bag_files file deletion

Test circular deletion with real storage (sqlite3/mcap) to verify
files are actually removed from disk when max_bag_files is exceeded.

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Simplify max_bag_files integration test setup and file extension check. Updated _storage stubs

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Fix message counts not adjusted during circular file deletion

When max_bag_files prunes old bag files, message_count and
topics_with_message_count were not decremented, showing full session
totals instead of only retained files.

Adds per-file per-topic tracking and adjusts counts on deletion.
Updates test to verify multi-topic scenario.

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Address PR #2218 review comments from MichaelOrlov

- Move implementation notes to Doxygen header comment
- Use std::deque instead of std::vector for per_file_topic_message_counts_
- Call finalize_metadata() before storage_->update_metadata() (2 locations)
- Use emplace_back() instead of push_back({})
- Add graceful error handling for fs::remove with std::error_code

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Address latest PR review comments from MichaelOrlov

Use C++ style Doxygen comments (///) for delete_oldest_files_if_needed
and move metadata adjustment into the else branch so it only runs when
file deletion succeeds.

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Fix delete_oldest_files_if_needed to always update metadata tracking

Refactor deletion logic to use a lambda that unconditionally removes
the oldest file from tracking (per-topic counts, metadata, file paths)
after each loop iteration. This prevents an infinite loop when
fs::exists returns false (e.g. in mock tests) or fs::remove fails,
while still correctly adjusting metadata regardless of deletion outcome.

Co-developed-by: MichaelOrlov
Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

* Keep failed-to-delete files in tracking, break to avoid tight loop

When fs::remove fails but the file still exists, keep it in metadata
and break out of the while loop. The deletion will be retried on the
next split. When the file doesn't exist on disk, remove from tracking
so the loop can proceed.

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

---------

Signed-off-by: Luke Sy <luke@usealloy.ai>

data.message:
Don't fail build if lsan isn't available (#397)

* Don't fail build if lsan isn't available

Since lsan isn't a required dependency for these packages, they
shouldn't fail to build when it isn't present.

Note that part of using a sanitizer involves additional libraries, so
using check_cxx_compiler_flag might not fail because it doesn't perform
linking like check_cxx_source_compiles does.

data.message:
Add option to disable recorder keyboard controls (#1607)

* added option to disable recorder keyboard controls

Signed-off-by: Bernd Pfrommer <bernd.pfrommer@gmail.com>

* Fixes per code review for disable keyboard controls for recorder

- Add CLI option --disable-keyboard-controls for rosbag2 recorder
- Instantiate KeyboardHandler with installed signal handler by default
in the recorded and player constructors to mitigate issues when CTRL+C
not handled on upper layers as when player and recorder constructed via
composition.
- Explicitly construct KeyboardHandler with disabled signal handler from
rosbag2_py layer since we have our own signals handler on that layer.
- Add test coverage for parameters parser for disable_keyboard_controls
parameters.

data.author: kylemarcey
data.message:
Merge "Add additional mode of publishing sim time updates (#1036)" to rolling  (#1050)

* Add new PlayOptions to control new method of /clock publishing

Signed-off-by: Kyle Marcey <kyle.marcey@apex.ai>

* Add new arguments to ros2bag frontend to support publishing /clock updates triggered by replayed messages

Signed-off-by: Kyle Marcey <kyle.marcey@apex.ai>

* Refactor rosbag2_transport::Player to allow two modes of publishing /clock updates.

An additional mode of operation has been added to the Player class to allow for /clock updates. When this mode is active, /clock updates are triggered whenever messages are replayed rather than at a fixed rate. Optionally, a list of triggering topics can be set so that only a subset of replayed messages will trigger the /clock update.

This mode is most useful when replaying applications which do some sanity checking or correlation of message data to system timestamps. If the application does not need the sim time to be updated at a consistent rate, this mode can substantially reduce the overhead of having sim time enabled in rosbag2.

Signed-off-by: Kyle Marcey <kyle.marcey@apex.ai>

data.author: theseankelly
data.message:
Make unpublished topics unrecorded by default (#968)

* Make unpublished topics unrecorded by default

Topics with no publishers do not offer any QoS profiles. This causes the
recorder to subscribe with a default QoS, which is often disadvantageous.

This change makes subscribing to unpublished topics optional, off by
default. Once a publisher is discovered for a topic, discovery picks it
up and adds it to the bag.

This addresses #967

Signed-off-by: Sean Kelly <skelly@seegrid.com>

* Feedback from review

Signed-off-by: Sean Kelly <skelly@seegrid.com>

* Additional review feedback

Signed-off-by: Sean Kelly <skelly@seegrid.com>

* Narrow scope of tests and make more deterministic

This is needed to try to fix Windows build

data.author: sriramster
data.message:
Fixes an init race condition (#93)

* This could probably be a race condition, for ex: When we've create a subscriber in the API, and the subscriber has the data already available in the callback (Cause of existing publishers) the db entry for the particular topic would not be availalble, which in turn returns an SqliteException. This is cause write_->create_topic() call is where we add the db entry for a particular topic. And, this leads to crashing before any recording.

Locally I solved it by adding the db entry first, and if
create_subscription fails, remove the topic entry from the db and also
erase the subscription.

Signed-off-by: Sriram Raghunathan <rsriram7@visteon.com>

data.author: Felix-El
data.message:
Indexing of messages.timestamp to avoid runtime-copy. (#121)

Extended SqliteStorage::initialize() to add an index for the message table's timestamp column.
Without this, the ORDER BY query in prepare_for_reading() causes expensive table duplication,
which also has potential for out-of-disk or out-of-memory errors.

data.author: greimela-si
data.message:
Use directory as bagfile and add additonal record options (#43)

* Use directory instead of file to reference bag files

* Extract process execution helper from e2e tests

* Prefix autogenerated bag name with `rosbag2_`

* GH-109 Improve e2e-test process handling on linux

- Use SIGINT instead of SIGTERM which is the correct equivalent to
  pressing Ctrl-C within a shell
- Use exec instead of system inside the forked test child process.
- Check the return code of the child after the waitpid instead of
  after the call to system.
Using system inside a child process is unnecessary as system forks
again. Whether the actual "ros2 bag" process was put in the same
process group seemed to be shell dependent. Exec avoids these issues.
